name: Bodyweight Lunge
resistance_profile: Hardest in the deep bottom position with the front knee and hip flexed — strong lengthened loading of the quads; easy near standing.
cues: Long step, drop the back knee
Push back up through the front foot
execution_steps: Stand tall with feet hip-width and hands on your hips.
Take a long step forward and lower until your back knee nearly touches the floor.
Keep your torso upright and your front knee tracking over your foot.
Push through the front foot to return to standing, then repeat on the other leg.
good_when: You're a beginner building single-leg control with no equipment.
You want a warm-up or higher-rep quad and glute movement at home.
You're evening out left/right differences early in training.
bad_when: You need heavy quad overload for hypertrophy (add load or use a squat).
Balance problems make free lunging unsafe (hold a support first).
